rulings.Case Title: ANDREW BAILEY VS. CHINATOWN COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CENTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Deposition Attendance Of Retained Experts Within Geographic Limits Of Ccp Section 2034.42
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday November 20, 2018 line 1. DEFENDANT CHINATOWN COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CENTER Notice Of Motion To Compel Deposition Attendance Of Retained Experts Within Geographic Limits Of Ccp Section 2034.42 DENIED. Per agreement (in writing) Defendant agreed to conduct expert witness depositions in San Diego. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ANTONAE ROBERTSON VS. THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL L. BROWN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Expert Witness Depositions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday November 20, 2018 line 7. DEFENDANT MELI BROWN, GWENDOLYN BROWN, PHILIP GRAY Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Expert Witness Depositions DENIED. Moving party provides no authority for the proposition that the requested relief of compelling Plaintiff's expert deposition based on untimely and insufficient Notice of Deposition is available. Plaintiff's reliance on the two cases cited in the Points and Authorities is misplaced. Neither cases is factually on point; neither addresses neglect to timely serve discovery requests; neither stands for the proposition that failure to propound timely discovery results in "judgment, dismissal, order, or other proceeding taken against him or her," a basis for CCP 473(b) relief. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ANTONAE ROBERTSON VS. THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL L. BROWN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Reopen Discovery For The Purpose Of Expert Witness Depositions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday November 20, 2018 line 7. DEFENDANT MELI BROWN, GWENDOLYN BROWN, PHILIP GRAY Notice Of Motion To Reopen Discovery For The Purpose Of Expert Witness Depositions DENIED. The trial in this action is on November 26, 2018. Meet and Confer declaration required by CCP 2024.050(a) is limited to one conclusory statement, which in no way states "facts showing a reasonable and good faith attempt at an informal resolution." CCP 2016.040. Re-opening discovery on the eve of trial is prejudicial to Plaintiff. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: THE SALVATORE ENTERPRISES, INC. VS. TWIN DRAGONS THAI, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday November 20, 2018 line 2. DEFENDANT TWIN DRAGONS THAI, INC., BURIN TRAKARNWUTIKOSOL, THANU CHAICHANA DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT sustained without leave to amend. Non-opposition filed. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: FAST CAPITAL, LLC. ET AL VS. LA COSTA LOANS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday November 20, 2018 line 3. DEFENDANT SS DREAMBUILDERS, LLC, CHONG HONG DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiffs' complaint is uncertain and fails to state facts to support each claim. In the amended complaint Plaintiff are to: (1) allege facts to establish standing of each Plaintiff to sue each defendant for each cause of action; (2) allege facts in support of each cause of each element of each cause of action; if liability is based on conspiracy, to so allege as to each cause of action; Plaintiff to make amendments in good faith and in light of authorities cited by Defendants as to each cause of action (3) to clarity whether Ps were lenders or investors; (4) to allege facts establishing that the recorded instruments, including post-foreclosure instruments are void or voidable and that cancellation of the instruments would result in Plaintiffs "title, their interests in and possession of the Subject Properties and Defendants' Other Properties." (FAC 57). The Court does not make any other rulings at this time. All other arguments may be raised by Defendants at a later time, if necessary and proper, after amends have been made. All amendments to be in bold font. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: FAST CAPITAL, LLC. ET AL VS. LA COSTA LOANS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday November 20, 2018 line 3. DEFENDANT LA COSTA LOANS, INC., RICHARD SHRIGLEY, SHRIGLEY HOLDINGS, INC. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiffs' complaint is uncertain and fails to state facts to support each claim. In the amended complaint Plaintiff are to: (1) allege facts to establish standing of each Plaintiff to sue each defendant for each cause of action; (2) allege facts in support of each cause of each element of each cause of action; if liability is based on conspiracy, to so allege as to each cause of action; Plaintiff to make amendments in good faith and in light of authorities cited by Defendants as to each cause of action (3) to clarity whether Ps were lenders or investors; (4) to allege facts establishing that the recorded instruments, including post-foreclosure instruments are void or voidable and that cancellation of the instruments would result in Plaintiffs "title, their interests in and possession of the Subject Properties and Defendants' Other Properties." (FAC 57). The Court does not make any other rulings at this time. All other arguments may be raised by Defendants at a later time, if necessary and proper, after amends have been made. All amendments to be in bold font. This is the first time the Court looks at this complaint. = (501/REQ)
